preamble: The voiced dental fricative /ð/ can undergo a process of lenition and vocalization, particularly in coda positions, leading to its transformation into the voiced labio-velar approximant /w/. This shift involves a weakening of the consonant and a change in its place of articulation.
phoneticEffects: Lenition (fricative to approximant), vocalization, and a shift in place of articulation from dental to labio-velar, specifically occurring in coda positions

examples.note: In Catalan, Latin intervocalic /d/ lenited to /ð/ in early Romance. This /ð/ subsequently lenited further to /w/ when it occurred in coda position, often due to syncope of a following vowel. An example is Latin 'CREDERE' becoming Catalan 'creure' ('to believe').

sources: Wheeler, M. W. (1998). Fonètica històrica i teoria fonològica. In Mas i Vives, J., Miralles i Montserrat, J., & Rosselló Bover, P. (Eds.), Actes de l'Onzè Col·loqui Internacional de Llengua i Literatura Catalanes (Vol. 1, pp. 299-337). Publicacions de l'Abadia de Montserrat.
